arts.ttl: Celtic nominated for another European award for off the pitch achievement
arts.sum: However, it seems that what the club are doing off the pitch in the local community is set to be recognised after Celtic were nominated for a prestigious award this afternoon. The ECA also recognised those clubs for not only their achievements on the pitch but also off it.

arts.ttl: Hatate problem, Engels dilemma and £5.5m wildcard – How Celtic will line up for Champions League opener
arts.sum: It's set to be a blockbuster night at Celtic Park as the Hoops take on Slovan Bratislava

arts.ttl: Michael Stewart explains why 'top talent' Luke McCowan could soon start for Celtic
arts.sum: Speaking on the Scottish Football Social Club, Stewart believes that competition in Celtic’s midfield could give McCowan a conceivable chance to establish himself in Brendan Rodgers’ starting line-up. Obviously, for Luke McCowan, being a Celtic supporter, I think Brendan Rodgers spoke about it himself after the game (saying) that it’s a special goal.

arts.ttl: 'I realised': Ange Postecoglou says what he learnt at Celtic after Tottenham's poor start to the season
arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou has evidently always been keen to uphold very high standards for his squads at both Celtic and Tottenham Hotspur whilst managing in the...

arts.ttl: Micah Richards asks Jamie Carragher if he prefers Celtic or Rangers, he doesn't hesitate
arts.sum: Celtic will begin their Champions League adventure against Slovan Bratislava on Wednesday, with American network CBS Sports providing coverage of the game for their viewers. But it’s not only Champions League games that CBS provide coverage for because you can also watch football from around the world, including Scotland’s top tier.

arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers 2.0 faces final Celtic frontier after 12 months of cajoling his own board – Keith Jackson
arts.sum: A target from the first press conference of Rodgers' second coming can finally be answered

arts.ttl: BBC pundit thinks Celtic have a 'huge' opportunity to make Champions League dent tonight
arts.sum: Celtic will receive elite billing tonight as they make their Champions League league phase bow against Slovan Bratislava this evening. Speaking to BBC Sport’s The Football News Show, former Premier League stalwart Brown has cited Celtic’s clash against Slovan Bratislava as a potentially ‘huge’ opportunity to get their Champions League campaign up and running.

arts.ttl: How much Celtic could bank from Champions League win or draw vs Slovan Bratislava
arts.sum: This season, the combined Champions League prize pool sits at a staggering £2.077billion and of course, that spells good news for Celtic. Get the latest news sent straight to your messages by joining our Celtic WhatsApp community today.

arts.ttl: Two of Celtic's Champions League opponents get destroyed on matchday one
arts.sum: And whilst the Celtic boss will take nothing for granted against Bratislava, a win would be a huge boost for Rodgers, especially after the hammerings Young Boys and Dinamo Zagreb took last night in their league phase opener. Young Boys opened up their Champions League campaign with a home tie against another of Celtic’s opponents, Aston Villa.

arts.ttl: The skyrocketing numbers behind Celtic's bid to create Champions League history with play-off only the start
arts.sum: The Hoops are huge outsiders to lift the famous trophy - but a spot in the knockouts has been handed a realistic chance
